campus: Department of Computer Science (Dipartimento di Informatica), Viale Regina Elena 295 / Città Universitaria, Rome [uncertain]
faculty: Information Engineering, Informatics and Statistics (Ingegneria dell'informazione, informatica e statistica); Department of Informatics
admission_test_type: Verification of requirements and personal preparation; assessment of academic knowledge (degree class, final grade, exams in relevant SSDs) with a possible interview; MoveIN pre-selection + interview for non-EU applicants
applicant_route: Italian qualification (Sapienza and other Italian universities)
non-EU residing in Italy (with residence permit)
non-EU residing abroad / Marco Polo (MoveIN pre-selection / visa-required)
application_platform: MoveIN pre-selection platform for non-EU applicants needing a visa; Infostud registration + application (PagoPA payment with course code 33508); Universitaly for visa pre-enrolment
access_quota_non_eu: Per-programme non-EU contingent is set in the official Call for Applications; not extracted in this pass [uncertain].
application_round: September 2026 intake. Non-EU (visa) MoveIN pre-selection 22 December 2025 - 15 May 2026; EU/equivalent until 31 July 2026. Programme requirements-assessment had multiple Infostud deadlines (for 2025/26: 16 Sep, 16 Oct, 18 Nov, 16 Dec, 15 Jan); equivalent 2026/2027 dates to be confirmed in the new call.
places_and_foreign_contingent: Total places and non-EU contingent published in the programme Call for Applications; not extracted in this pass [uncertain].

academic_requirements.summary: A degree (or equivalent foreign qualification) with at least 90 ECTS in the listed scientific-disciplinary sectors (SSDs): INF/01; ING-INF/01-05; FIS/01, FIS/02, FIS/07; MAT/01, MAT/02, MAT/03, MAT/05, MAT/06, MAT/07, MAT/08, MAT/09; SECS-S/01, SECS-S/06. Among these, at least 72 ECTS must be in foundation/core subjects (mathematics, physics, computer science, ICT, data science, information engineering).
academic_requirements.required_knowledge: Programming languages; algorithms and data structures; computer architectures; databases; computer networks; operating systems; computability and complexity theory.
academic_requirements.assessment: Evaluated on degree class (L-31 or L-8), final grade, and exam performance in relevant SSDs. Holders of an L-31 or L-8 degree who graduated with at least 100/110 are generally admitted without interview; otherwise an interview may be required.
academic_requirements.source: sapienzastudents.net Computer Science enrolment page (sourced from official attachment); MoveIN academic requirements 2026-2027

country_specific_eligibility: Foreign qualifications must be valid per Sapienza's 'Accepted qualifications by country' (2026/2027); equivalent foreign SSDs accepted for the 90/72 ECTS rule. MoveIN minimum GPA 75/100 for non-EU applicants. https://www.uniroma1.it/sites/default/files/field_file_allegati/accepted_qualifications_by_country_26mag26.pdf
curricular_integration_policy: If the 90/72 ECTS requirements are not met, missing credits can be acquired by passing Single Courses; methods and costs are set out in Article 41 of the Sapienza Student Regulations.
conditional_enrolment_policy: An additional January deadline is reserved for students graduating at Sapienza (e.g. 15 January 2026 for the 2025/26 cycle); candidates who have not yet graduated may apply for the requirements assessment, with final admission conditional on degree completion. Candidates from other Italian universities must email the Didactic Committee (commissionedidattica@di.uniroma1.it) by the same deadlines.

accepted_certificates_and_scores: IELTS (academic) 5.5 or higher (incl. IELTS Academic Home-Based Test)
TOEFL iBT 80 or higher (incl. TOEFL iBT Home Edition)
Cambridge English B2 First or higher (also CAE, CPE)
Trinity College London ISE II or higher
PTE General Level 3 (B2) or higher / PTE Academic
language_requirements.alternative: If no valid certificate is provided, B2 may be verified via an interview, as determined by the Committee.
language_requirements.waivers: Waived for native English speakers; holders of an English-taught diploma/degree from an accredited institution in EU/EEA/Schengen, Australia, Canada, New Zealand, the UK or the US; holders of International Baccalaureate, GCE/GCSE or comparable. Fully waived for applicants from UK, US, Canada, Ireland, Australia, New Zealand.
language_requirements.source: sapienzastudents.net Computer Science enrolment page; MoveIN academic requirements 2026-2027; English language requirements 2026 PDF

language_submission_timing: Submitted at MoveIN pre-selection (non-EU) and/or with the Infostud requirements-assessment application; verified by interview if no certificate is provided.
italian_language_requirement: Not required for admission; programme taught entirely in English. Italian useful for daily life [uncertain].
application_deadline_eu: EU and EU-equivalent candidates (no visa) may apply until 31 July 2026 for 2026/2027. The programme's requirements-assessment step has multiple Infostud deadlines (for 2025/26: 16 Oct, 18 Nov, 16 Dec, 15 Jan reserved for Sapienza graduating students); 2026/2027 dates to be confirmed in the new call. [uncertain on exact 2026/2027 programme dates]
application_deadline_non_eu: Non-EU candidates residing abroad and requiring a visa: MoveIN pre-selection 22 December 2025 - 15 May 2026 (for 2025/26 the only Infostud requirements-assessment date for visa applicants was 16 September 2025). Pre-accepted students must register on Universitaly by 30 June 2026.
application_fee: EUR 30 per submitted application via MoveIN (max 2 applications per candidate per academic year; no waiver). The programme's Infostud requirements-assessment step had a EUR 10 fee (PagoPA, course code 33508) for the 2025/26 cycle.
visa_preenrolment_deadline: Pre-accepted non-EU students must pre-enrol on Universitaly by 30 June 2026.
required_documents: Transcript of records (with exams and ECTS in relevant SSDs)
English language certificate (or interview if none)
Additional supporting documents as requested in the programme call
document_translation_authentication: Foreign documents must comply with Sapienza's 'Accepted qualifications by country' rules; legalization / Declaration of Value / CIMEA statement may be required for foreign qualifications [uncertain on programme-specific detail].

entry_exam_or_test.type: No standardized written entrance exam. Admission by verification of requirements and personal preparation: assessment of academic knowledge (degree class, final grade, SSD exams); applicants not meeting the 'L-31/L-8 with >=100/110, admitted without interview' condition may be called for an interview. Non-EU applicants additionally pass MoveIN pre-selection.
entry_exam_or_test.source: sapienzastudents.net Computer Science enrolment page; catalogue page (Access procedure: Verification of requirements and personal preparation)

test_variant_and_session: Interview held only when the no-interview condition is not met; scheduled individually, no fixed public test session [uncertain].
test_score_conversion_or_threshold: Automatic admission without interview for L-31/L-8 degree holders with final grade >= 100/110. Academic requirement: 90 ECTS in listed SSDs (>=72 in core/foundation). MoveIN minimum GPA 75/100; English minimum IELTS 5.5 / TOEFL iBT 80.

source_quotes.quote: Programme code 33508 ... Course type Master's degree ... Bachelor class LM-18 ... Access procedure Verification of requirements and personal preparation ... Faculty Ingegneria dell'informazione, informatica e statistica ... Department INFORMATICA ... Language English

you must hold a degree (or equivalent foreign qualification) and have obtained at least 90 ECTS in the following scientific-disciplinary sectors ... Among these 90 ECTS, at least 72 must be in core subjects ... If you hold an L-31 or L-8 degree and graduated with at least 100/110, you are generally admitted without an interview.

A knowledge of English at B2 level or higher is mandatory. You must either provide valid certification or attend an interview ... you can submit your application and pay the EUR 10 fee on Infostud ... Payment must be made via PagoPA using course code 33508
Please note that candidates must have acquired at least 90 ECTS in the scientific-disciplinary sectors (SSDs) below ... MINIMUM GPA: 75/100 ... IELTS 5.5 or higher ... TOEFL iBT 80 or higher

uncertainty_notes: The task's official_program_url (/en/course/33516) renders the Computer Science programme; the catalogue internal programme code shown on that page is 33508 and class LM-18, Department of Informatics. (The /corso/2024/29932/home path instead renders Cybersecurity / code 33516, so the task URLs for the Computer Science and Cybersecurity programmes appear swapped.)

Specific programme deadlines and the EUR 10 Infostud requirements-assessment fee are taken from the 2025/2026 cycle (student-maintained page sourced from the official attachment); 2026/2027 dates must be confirmed in the new call.
Per-programme non-EU access quota and total places not extracted.
Campus building inferred from Department of Informatics location; not explicitly stated on the programme page.
Document authentication (Declaration of Value / CIMEA) requirement inferred from general Sapienza policy.
Italian language requirement assumed not required (English-taught), not explicitly stated.
faculty_call_pdf_url found via search as the official documenti_ufficiali bando PDF for code 33508; year/edition should be verified.
